message:
fix(mail): Use town beads for all mail (two-level architecture)

Replace complex address-based routing with simple town root lookup.
All mail goes to ~/gt/.beads/ (town beads), eliminating the broken
rig-level routing.

Two-level model:
- Town beads (~/gt/.beads/): ALL mail and coordination
- Clone beads (<rig>/crew/*/.beads/): Project issues only

Fixes: gt-4qey

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"gt-2kz","title":"CLI: cleanup commands for stale state","description":"Cleanup commands for recovering from stale state.

## Commands

### gt cleanup <polecat>
Clean stale state for specific polecat.
```
gt cleanup <rig>/<polecat> [--dry-run]
```

Actions:
- Remove orphaned state.json if clone missing
- Clear stale session references
- Reset stuck state (working → idle after timeout)

### gt cleanup --all
Clean all polecats in workspace.
```
gt cleanup --all [--dry-run]
```

## Implementation
```go
func CleanupPolecat(rigName, polecatName string, dryRun bool) (*CleanupResult, error)

type CleanupResult struct {
    OrphanedStateRemoved bool
    SessionsCleared      int
    StateReset           bool
    Warnings             []string
}
```

## Overlap with Doctor
- Doctor diagnoses and offers --fix
- Cleanup is more aggressive state recovery
- Consider merging into doctor --fix

## New File
internal/cmd/cleanup.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Removes orphaned state files
- [ ] Clears stale session refs
- [ ] --dry-run shows what would happen
- [ ] Reports all actions taken","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:48:31.944982-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:07:12.430696-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-4nn.2","title":"Molecule instantiation: create child beads from template","description":"When instantiating a molecule on a work bead:

## Transaction Flow

1. Parse molecule's `## Step:` sections from description
2. Begin SQLite transaction
3. For each step, create child issue:
   - ID: `{parent-id}.{step-ref}` or generated
   - Title: step title (from header or first line)
   - Description: step prose instructions
   - Type: task
   - Priority: inherit from parent
4. Add `instantiated-from` edge from each step to molecule:
   ```sql
   INSERT INTO dependencies (issue_id, depends_on_id, type, metadata)
   VALUES (step_id, mol_id, 'instantiated-from', '{"step": "implement"}');
   ```
5. Wire inter-step dependencies from `Needs:` lines
6. Commit transaction (atomic - all or nothing)

## Parsing Conventions

```markdown
## Step: <ref>
<prose instructions>
Needs: <step>, <step>  # optional
Tier: haiku|sonnet|opus  # optional hint
```

## Parameterization

Steps can have `{{variable}}` placeholders:
```markdown
## Step: implement
Implement {{feature_name}} in {{target_file}}.
```

Context map provided at instantiation time.

## API

```go
func (s *Store) InstantiateMolecule(mol *Issue, parent *Issue, ctx map[string]string) ([]*Issue, error)
func ParseMoleculeSteps(description string) ([]MoleculeStep, error)
```

Implementation lives in `internal/beads/molecule.go`.","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:52.071066-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T01:57:17.028198-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T01:57:17.028198-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4nn.2","depends_on_id":"gt-4nn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:52.072554-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-4nn.2","depends_on_id":"gt-4nn.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:07:02.949242-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-4nn.3","title":"Molecule CLI: bd molecule commands","description":"Add molecule commands to bd:

## Commands

```bash
bd molecule list              # List molecules (type: molecule)
bd molecule show <id>         # Show molecule with parsed steps
bd molecule parse <id>        # Validate and show parsed structure  
bd molecule instantiate <mol-id> --parent=<issue-id>  # Create steps
bd molecule instances <mol-id>  # Show all instantiations
```

## gt spawn integration

```bash
gt spawn --issue <id> --molecule <mol-id>
```

This should:
1. Call `bd molecule instantiate` (creates child beads atomically)
2. Spawn polecat on first ready step
3. Polecat grinds through via `bd ready`

## Output Examples

```
$ bd molecule show mol-abc

mol-abc: Engineer in a Box
Type: molecule

Steps (5):
  design      → (ready first)
  implement   → Needs: design
  review      → Needs: implement
  test        → Needs: implement  
  submit      → Needs: review, test
  
Instances: 3
```

```
$ bd molecule instances mol-abc

Parent          Status    Created
gt-xyz          done      2025-12-15
gt-abc          active    2025-12-17 (3/5 complete)
gt-def          pending   2025-12-18
```","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:53.919884-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T12:00:37.165927-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T12:00:37.165927-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4nn.3","depends_on_id":"gt-4nn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:53.921621-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-4nn.3","depends_on_id":"gt-4nn.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:07:03.048941-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-4nn.4","title":"Built-in molecules: engineer-in-box, quick-fix, research","description":"Create built-in molecules as Beads issues:

## engineer-in-box

```markdown
id: mol-engineer-in-box
type: molecule
title: Engineer in a Box

Full workflow from design to merge.

## Step: design
Think carefully about architecture. Consider:
- Existing patterns in the codebase
- Trade-offs between approaches  
- Testability and maintainability

Write a brief design summary before proceeding.

## Step: implement
Write the code. Follow codebase conventions.
Needs: design

## Step: review
Self-review the changes. Look for:
- Bugs and edge cases
- Style issues
- Missing error handling
Needs: implement

## Step: test
Write and run tests. Cover happy path and edge cases.
Fix any failures before proceeding.
Needs: implement

## Step: submit
Submit for merge via refinery.
Needs: review, test
```

## quick-fix

```markdown
id: mol-quick-fix
type: molecule  
title: Quick Fix

Fast path for small changes.

## Step: implement
Make the fix. Keep it focused.

## Step: test
Run relevant tests. Fix any regressions.
Needs: implement

## Step: submit
Submit for merge.
Needs: test
```

## research

```markdown
id: mol-research
type: molecule
title: Research

Investigation workflow.

## Step: investigate
Explore the question. Search code, read docs, 
understand context. Take notes.

## Step: document
Write up findings. Include:
- What you learned
- Recommendations
- Open questions
Needs: investigate
```

## Storage

Built-in molecules live in `<town>/.beads/` as regular issues.
Created during `gt install` or `bd init`.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:07:04.574565-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T12:02:19.332406-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T12:02:19.332406-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4nn.4","depends_on_id":"gt-4nn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:07:04.576587-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-53w6","title":"Witness MVP: Automated Polecat Lifecycle","description":"Implement the Witness agent - per-rig 'pit boss' that manages polecat lifecycles.

The Witness enables hands-free swarming by automating:
- Spawning polecats for ready work
- Monitoring worker health
- Processing shutdown requests  
- Cleaning up worktrees when done
- Escalating stuck workers to Mayor

Without Witness, humans must manually spawn, monitor, and kill each polecat.

## Core Loop

```
while True:
  # Handle pending shutdowns
  for polecat in polecats where state == pending_shutdown:
    verify git clean
    kill session  
    remove worktree
    delete branch
    
  # Spawn for ready work
  ready = bd ready --parent=<epic> if epic else bd ready
  for issue in ready:
    if active_workers < max_workers:
      gt spawn --issue <id>
      
  # Check worker health
  for polecat in active polecats:
    if stuck (no progress for 30 min):
      nudge or escalate
      
  sleep 60
```

## Blocking Bugs to Fix
- gt-dsfi: handoff deadlock
- gt-n7z7: refinery foreground race condition
- gm-c6b: mail coordination (town vs rig beads)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:13:45.075731-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T09:32:16.49265-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T09:32:16.49265-08:00","close_reason":"All MVP subtasks complete: witness start, shutdown handler, auto-spawn, health checks"}
@@ -100,6 +102,7 @@
 {"id":"gt-7ik","title":"Ephemeral polecats: spawn fresh, delete on completion","description":"## Design Decision

Switch from pooled/idle polecats to ephemeral model:
- Spawn creates fresh worktree from main
- Polecat requests shutdown when done (bottom-up)
- Witness verifies handoff, kills session, deletes worktree
- No 'idle' state - polecats exist only while working

## Rationale

1. **Git worktrees are fast** - pooling optimization is obsolete
2. **Pooling creates maintenance burden:**
   - Git stashes accumulate
   - Untracked artifacts pile up
   - Branches drift from main
   - Beads DB gets stale
3. **PGT sync problems** came from persistent branches
4. **Support infrastructure exists** - Witness, Refinery, Mayor handle continuity
5. **Simpler mental model** - polecat exists = work in progress

## Lifecycle

```
Spawn:
  gt spawn --issue <id>
  → Creates fresh worktree: git worktree add polecats/<name> -b polecat/<name>
  → Initializes beads in worktree
  → Starts session, assigns work

Working:
  Polecat does task
  → Pushes to polecat/<name> branch
  → Submits to merge queue when ready

Completion (POLECAT-INITIATED):
  Polecat runs: gt handoff
  → Verifies git state clean
  → Sends mail to Witness: "Ready for shutdown"
  → Marks itself done, waits for termination

Cleanup (WITNESS-OWNED):
  Witness receives shutdown request
  → Verifies PR merged or in queue
  → Verifies no uncommitted changes
  → Kills session: gt session stop <rig>/<polecat>
  → Deletes worktree: git worktree remove polecats/<name>
  → Deletes branch: git branch -d polecat/<name>
  → Optionally: Notifies Mayor of completion
```

## Key Insight: Bottom-Up Shutdown

**Old model (wrong)**: Top-down batch shutdown - "cancel the swarm"
**New model (right)**: Bottom-up individual shutdown - polecat requests, Witness executes

This enables streaming:
- Workers come and go continuously
- No "swarm end" to trigger cleanup
- Each worker manages its own lifecycle
- Witness is the lifecycle authority

## Implementation

1. Add `gt handoff` command for polecats to request shutdown
2. Modify gt spawn to always create fresh worktree
3. Run bd init in new worktree (beads needs initialization)
4. Add shutdown request handler to Witness
5. Witness verifies handoff, then cleans up:
   - Kill session
   - Remove worktree
   - Delete branch
6. Remove 'idle' state from polecat state machine
7. Simplify gt polecat list (only shows active)

## Impact on Other Tasks

- gt-17r (Zombie cleanup): Becomes trivial - orphan worktrees
- gt-4my (Worker health): Simpler - no idle/stuck ambiguity
- gt-f9x.5/f9x.6 (Doctor): Fewer states to validate
- gt-eu9 (Witness handoff): Witness receives polecat shutdown requests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-17T15:44:31.139964-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T01:57:17.033547-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T01:57:17.033547-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-7lt","title":"gt mail send should tmux-notify recipient","description":"## Problem

When mail is sent via gt mail send, the recipient session does not get a tmux notification. In Python Gas Town (PGT), mail delivery triggers a tmux display-message or similar notification so the agent knows mail arrived.

## Expected Behavior

When gt mail send <addr> is called:
1. Mail is delivered to recipient inbox
2. If recipient has an active tmux session, send notification
3. Notification should be visible (display-message or bell)

## Current Behavior

Mail is delivered but no notification. Agent has to poll inbox to discover new mail.

## Impact

- Agents miss time-sensitive messages
- Heartbeat pokes from daemon will not wake agents
- Coordination is slower (polling vs push)

## Implementation Notes

After successful mail delivery, check if recipient has active session:
- gt session list can identify active sessions
- tmux display-message or send-keys can notify
- Could inject a visible prompt like "[MAIL] New message from <sender>"

## Reference

Check PGT implementation for how it handles this.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T12:28:50.142075-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T20:09:53.112902-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T20:09:53.112902-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-7o7","title":"Session pre-shutdown checks","description":"Session stop should verify clean state before killing, like PGT.

## Pre-Shutdown Checks

### 1. Git Working Tree Clean
```go
func checkGitClean(clonePath string) error {
    // git status --porcelain
    // Fail if any output
}
```

### 2. All Commits Pushed
```go
func checkCommitsPushed(clonePath string) error {
    // git log origin/HEAD..HEAD
    // Fail if any unpushed commits
}
```

### 3. Assigned Issues Handled
```go
func checkIssuesHandled(polecat *Polecat) error {
    // If polecat.Issue != "", check if closed or reassigned
}
```

### 4. Beads Synced
```go
func checkBeadsSynced(clonePath string) error {
    // bd sync --status in clone directory
}
```

## Behavior on Failure
1. First attempt: Nudge worker to fix
2. Retry up to 3 times with delay
3. After retries: Escalate to Witness/Mayor

## Integration
Modify internal/session/manager.go Stop():
```go
func (m *Manager) Stop(polecat string, force bool) error {
    if !force {
        if err := m.runPreShutdownChecks(polecat); err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("pre-shutdown checks failed: %w", err)
        }
    }
    // existing stop logic
}
```

## Flags
- --force: Skip checks
- --grace-period N: Time to wait for fixes

## Dependencies
- Ties into gt-69l (hook system) - can be hook-based
- Ties into gt-f8v (Witness pre-kill verification)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Stop fails if uncommitted changes (without --force)
- [ ] Stop fails if unpushed commits
- [ ] Clear error messages with fix instructions
- [ ] --force bypasses all checks","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:47:55.968983-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:05:02.795812-08:00"}
+{"id":"gt-7oow","title":"gt mail: Cross-level routing is broken","description":"**Problem:**
When Mayor (at ~/gt) sends mail to a rig worker (gastown/crew/max), the message lands in town-level beads (~/.beads/) but the recipient checks rig-level beads (crew/max/.beads/).

**Reproduction:**
```bash
cd ~/gt
gt mail send gastown/crew/max -s 'Test' -m 'body'
# Message goes to ~/gt/.beads/ with prefix hq-*

cd ~/gt/gastown/crew/max  
gt mail inbox
# Does NOT see the message - it's looking in crew/max/.beads/
```

**Root cause:**
`findBeadsWorkDir()` walks up from CWD to find .beads. `Router.Send()` runs `bd create` in that directory. This means messages always go to the sender's beads, not the recipient's.

**Fix options:**
1. Route based on recipient address - if sending to rig/*, use that rig's .beads
2. Use a single shared beads database for all mail (simpler but less isolated)
3. Teach agents to check both levels (workaround, not fix)

**Related:**
- gt-ngu1: Pinned beads sorting (done but pointless if mail doesn't route)
- This blocks all cross-level mail functionality","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:57:30.258991-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T17:57:30.258991-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-7we","title":"Swarms of One: Lightweight single-worker task dispatch","description":"Design and implement a lightweight pattern for firing off single workers to handle tasks without full swarm overhead.

## Context

Currently we have:
- town spawn: Creates a polecat in a rig with issue assignment
- Swarms (sw-*): Full lifecycle tracking with manifest, state, events, reports
- Ephemeral rigs (rig-*): Temporary worker groups for swarms

What's missing: A simple way to say 'fire off a worker to do X' without swarm ceremony.

## Design Questions

1. Should this be a new command like 'gt fire' or an option on existing commands?
2. Should single tasks still get swarm IDs (sw-N) for consistency/queryability?
3. Should it default to creating new workers or support --reuse for idle polecats?
4. How does this relate to crew workers (gt-cik)?

## Possible Interface

gt fire --rig gastown --issue gt-xyz [--prompt '...']
gt fire gastown/QuickTask --issue gt-xyz

## Related

- gt-cik: Overseer Crew (user-managed persistent workspaces)
- gt-kmn: Swarm System epic","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T16:51:02.716629-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:18.589027-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:18.589027-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-82y","title":"Design: Swarm shutdown and worker cleanup","description":"Design for graceful swarm shutdown, worker cleanup, and session cycling.

## Key Decisions

1. Pre-kill verification uses model intelligence (not framework rules)
2. Witness can request restart when context filling (mail self, exit)
3. Mayor NOT involved in per-worker cleanup (Witness responsibility)
4. Clear responsibility boundaries between Mayor/Witness/Polecat

## Subtasks (implementation)

- gt-sd6: Polecat decommission checklist prompting
- gt-f8v: Witness pre-kill verification protocol
- gt-eu9: Witness session cycling and handoff
- gt-gl2: Mayor vs Witness cleanup responsibilities

**Design complete.** Each subtask has full specification in its description.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:47:44.936374-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:49:22.849598-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-15T20:12:05.441911-08:00"}
@@ -300,7 +303,7 @@
 {"id":"gt-n7z7","title":"Bug: refinery --foreground detects parent session as already running","description":"When gt refinery start gastown runs:
1. Creates tmux session gt-gastown-refinery
2. Sends 'gt refinery start gastown --foreground' into the session
3. The foreground command checks HasSession() - finds the session it's inside
4. Returns 'already running' error

The foreground mode check should either:
- Skip the tmux session check (only check PID)
- Use a different indicator that the daemon loop is running
- Pass a flag to indicate we're being called from the background starter

Workaround: Manually run 'gt refinery start gastown --foreground' from a fresh session.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:56:20.326369-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T03:52:11.20518-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T03:52:11.20518-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed: Added timeout warnings to handoff wait, fixed refinery foreground race condition"}
 {"id":"gt-nam3","title":"Update docs to reflect molecule-first paradigm","description":"Gas Town is fundamentally a molecule execution engine. Documentation should reflect this more clearly.

## Issues Found

### 1. gt spawn examples show molecule as optional
README.md line 116: `gt spawn --issue <id> # Start polecat on issue`
Should emphasize: polecats execute molecules, not just issues.

### 2. Architecture.md spawn examples inconsistent
Line 344 shows molecule: `gt spawn --issue gt-xyz --molecule mol-engineer-in-box`
Line 1434 shows without: `gt spawn --issue <id>`

### 3. Config vs molecule distinction not clear
outposts.yaml shows static policy - should note when molecules apply.

### 4. Operational molecules section is good but buried
Lines 430-566 cover operational molecules well. Should be more prominent.

## Updates Needed
- [ ] README: Update spawn examples to show molecule usage
- [ ] architecture.md: Ensure all spawn examples include molecules
- [ ] architecture.md: Add section on "when config vs molecule"
- [ ] architecture.md: Move operational molecules higher in document
- [ ] Add principle: "If it requires cognition, it's a molecule"
- [ ] federation-design.md: Note that policy can escalate to mol-outpost-assign

## Key Message
Gas Town doesn't spawn workers on issues. It spawns workers on molecules.
The issue is just the seed data for the molecule execution.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:26:31.842406-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T09:26:21.813833-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T09:26:21.813833-08:00","close_reason":"Updated docs with molecule-first paradigm - all spawn examples include molecules, added Config vs Molecule section with cognition principle, updated federation-design.md with policy escalation note"}

Run 'bd show gt-ogr' for details.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/slit","created_at":"2025-12-20T07:53:01.093695-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T07:59:51.445668-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T07:59:51.445668-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed rig count logic by adding extractRigFromSession() to handle both witness naming patterns (gt-<rig>-witness and gt-witness-<rig>), and isPolecatSession() to exclude witness/refinery/crew from polecat count"}
-{"id":"gt-ngu1","title":"Pinned beads should appear first in mail inbox","description":"Currently bd mail inbox sorts by priority then date, but pinned beads (handoff context) should always appear at the top.

**Current behavior:**
- Pinned beads mixed in with regular mail based on priority/date

**Expected behavior:**
- Pinned beads always first in inbox (before priority sorting)
- This enables handoff beads to be the default first item an agent sees

**Implementation:**
1. In mail.go runMailInbox(), after filtering, sort pinned beads to top
2. Within pinned and non-pinned groups, maintain priority/date sort

**Related:**
- gt-r8ej: Implement pinned beads for handoff state
- gt-8h4: Pinned Beads epic

**Acceptance criteria:**
- bd mail inbox shows pinned beads first
- Pinned beads still sorted by priority/date among themselves
- Non-pinned mail sorted normally after pinned section","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:45:24.133521-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T17:45:24.133521-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-ngu1","depends_on_id":"gt-8h4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:45:31.978176-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-ni6a","title":"Witness role template + CLAUDE.md","description":"Create the Witness agent's role context:

   - Startup protocol (gt prime, check inbox)

The Witness is the per-rig 'pit boss' - NOT a global coordinator.  {"id":"gt-njr","title":"Engineer session restart protocol","description":"Implement session restart flow for when the Engineer needs to split work:

1. Engineer creates subtask(s) in Beads assigned to self
2. Engineer sends handoff mail to self (🤝 HANDOFF)
3. Engineer sends restart request to Witness
4. Witness verifies:
   - Handoff mail exists in Engineer outbox/sent
   - Subtasks filed in Beads
5. Witness restarts the Refinery session (new Engineer context)

This enables "occasionally consistent, eventually convergent" work patterns.
The Refinery continues; the Engineer gets fresh context.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:02:48.22994-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T23:07:42.05363-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-njr","depends_on_id":"gt-h5n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:02:56.148564-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
